title: PHYSECON
text:
Potential is “intrinsic energy of inertia” label \[\begin{split} &
\nabla \cdot \epsilon_0 \mathscr{E} = \varrho \qquad
\nabla \cdot \mathscr{B} = 0
\newline &
\nabla \times \mathscr{E} = -\frac{\partial}{\partial t} \mathscr{B} \qquad
\nabla \times \frac{1}{\mu_0} \mathscr{B} = j + \epsilon_0 \frac{\partial}{\partial t} \mathscr{E}
\newline &
\mathscr{B} = \nabla \times \boldsymbol{U_m} \qquad
\mathscr{E} = -\nabla U_e - \frac{\partial}{\partial t} \boldsymbol{U_m}
\end{split} \tag{1}\]
\(\Rightarrow\) Wave equation for potential: \[\begin{split} &
\Delta \cdot U - \frac{1}{c^2} \frac{\partial^2}{\partial t^2} U = J
\end{split} \tag{2}\]
\(\Rightarrow\) Potential by “ignition J” (cause, inhomgenity) from source at rest, v = 0: \[\begin{split} &
U = -\frac{1}{4\pi} \int \frac{J}{\left | r  \right |} \, dr'
\end{split} \tag{3}\]
elec. Potential structure: \(J = -\frac{1}{\epsilon_0} \varrho\) \[\begin{split} &
U_e \coloneqq \Phi
\newline &
\stackrel{\textnormal{elec. charge dens.=}\varrho}{=}
\frac{1}{4\pi} \frac{1}{\epsilon_0} \int \frac{\varrho}{\left | r  \right |} \, dr'
\newline &
\> \hat{=} \>
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left | r \right |} \frac{1}{\epsilon_0} Q =
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left | r \right |} \frac{1}{\epsilon_0} It
\end{split} \tag{4}\]
magn. Potential structure: \(J = -\mu_0 j\) \[\begin{split} &
U_m \coloneqq \boldsymbol{A}
\newline &
\stackrel{\textnormal{elec. current dens.=}j}{=}
\frac{\mu_0}{4\pi} \int \frac{j}{\left | r  \right |} \, dr'
\newline &
\> \hat{=} \>
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left | r \right |} \mu_0 I =
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left | r \right |} \frac{1}{c^2 \epsilon_0} I =
\frac{1}{c^2} \frac{1}{t} U_e
\newline &
\stackrel{\textnormal{source in uniformal movement,} v = const. \neq 0}{=}
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left( \left | r \right | - r\cdot\frac{v}{c} \right)} \mu_0 Qv
\newline &
=
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left( \left | r \right | - r\cdot\frac{v}{c} \right)} \frac{1}{c^2 \epsilon_0} vQ
\newline &
=
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left( \left | r \right | - r\cdot\frac{v}{c} \right)} \frac{1}{c^2 \epsilon_0} Itv =
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left( \left | r \right | - r\cdot\frac{v}{c} \right)} \frac{1}{c^2 \epsilon_0} Ir
\end{split} \tag{5}\]


Electric endogen energy \(E_{pot} = mc^2\):
\[\begin{split} &
e = \textnormal{electron charge} \qquad
q = ne = \textnormal{charge} \qquad
n \in \mathbb{N}
\end{split} \tag{6}\]
\[\begin{split} &
\textnormal{Potential} \qquad
U =
\frac{E_{pot}}{q} =
\frac{\left( mc^2 \right)^2}{ne} =
\frac{m}{q} m c^4 =
\kappa m c^4
\end{split} \tag{7}\]
Electric induced energy (exogenous excitation) \(E_{kin} = \frac{1}{2}mv^2 \> \hat{=} \> \left( pc \right)^2\):
\(E_{kin}\) Energy density electric: \[\begin{split} &
w_e = \frac{1}{2} \epsilon_0 \mathscr{E}^2
\end{split} \tag{8}\]
\(E_{kin}\) Energy density magnetic: \[\begin{split} &
w_m = \frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{\mu_0} \mathscr{B}^2
\end{split} \tag{9}\]
\(E_{kin}\) Electric Energy: \[\begin{split} &
W_e = \frac{1}{2} CU^2
\end{split} \tag{10}\]
\(E_{kin}\) Magnetic Energy: \[\begin{split} &
W_m = \frac{1}{2} LI^2
\end{split} \tag{11}\]
Power: \[\begin{split} &
P = VI = (U_1 - U_2) \frac{Q}{t} =
\frac{E_1 - E_2}{t} = \frac{W}{t} = \frac{Fr}{t} = Fv
\end{split} \tag{12}\]
Conductor (charge conductive element for energy transfer) condition: \[\begin{split} &
\sigma \cdot t \gg \epsilon_0
\end{split} \tag{13}\]
Slow changing current (quasi-stationary, low frequencies) condition: \[\begin{split} &
\frac{v}{c} \ll \frac{1}{c}\frac{\mathscr{E}}{\mathscr{B}} \ll \frac{c}{v}
\end{split} \tag{14}\]
Energy conservation for (large extended) conductive medium: \[\begin{split} &   
\int    \left(
\frac{\partial}{\partial t} \left( we + w_m \right) + \mathbf{\nabla} \cdot \mathbf{\mathscr{S}} + \boldsymbol{j} \cdot \mathscr{E}
\right) \, dr
= \frac{d}{dt}W_e + \frac{d}{dt}W_m + D_S + \int \left( \boldsymbol{j} \cdot \mathscr{E} \right) \, dr
\newline &
\stackrel{\textnormal{low frequency}}{=}
\frac{1}{2}\frac{d}{dt} \left( LI^2 \right) + \frac{1}{2}\frac{d}{dt} \left( \frac{Q^2}{C} \right) + \left( RI^2 - VI \right) = 0
\end{split} \tag{15}\]
Energy of thin conductor (concentrated conductive medium without electric charge in its surrounding), Voltage (potentialdifference, tension):
\[\begin{split} &
V =
U_1 - U_2 =
r \mathscr{E} =
\dot{\Psi} + R I + \frac{I}{C}t =
L \dot{I} + R I + \frac{Q}{C}
\end{split} \tag{16}\]
\[
\Delta \cdot \coloneqq \textnormal{ div grad = Laplacian}
\]\[  
J = \textnormal{"ignition of inhomogenity,cause"} \quad
j = \textnormal{Current density} \quad
\varrho = \textnormal{Charge density}
\]\[  
V = \textnormal{Potentialdifference} \quad  
I = \textnormal{Current} \quad
R = \textnormal{Resistance} \quad
\]\[  
L = \textnormal{Inductance} \qquad  
C = \textnormal{Capacitance} \qquad
Q = c\textnormal{harge (ion)} \qquad
\Psi = LI = \textnormal{mag. Flux}
\]\[
\mathscr{S} = \mathscr{E} \times \mathscr{H} = \textnormal{Poynting-Vector = radiation intensity}
\]\[
D_S = \int \left( \mathbf{\nabla} \cdot \mathbf{\mathscr{S}} \right) \, dr = \int \textnormal{div}(\mathscr{E} \times \mathscr{H}) \, dr = \textnormal{dissipation engergy lost from radiation} \approx \textnormal{0 for low frequencies } \omega
\]\[
d = \sqrt{\frac{1}{\mu \sigma} \frac{2}{\omega}} = \textnormal{skin depth (surface) of conductor} \qquad
r = \frac{d}{\sqrt{2i}}\rho = \frac{d}{1+i}\rho = \textnormal{radius (extension) of conductor}
\]\[
\rho = kr = \textnormal{"radius of curvature $k$"}  \qquad
\omega = 2 \pi \nu = k \lambda \frac{1}{T}  = \sqrt{\frac{k}{m}} = \frac{v}{r}
\> \hat{=} \>
\frac{1}{\sqrt{LC}}
\> \hat{=} \>
\sqrt{\frac{g}{r}}
= \textnormal{frequency}
\]\[
\mu = \textnormal{permeability of conductor}    \qquad
\sigma = \textnormal{conductivity of conductor}
\]


Low Frequency: \[\begin{split} & \omega \ll \frac{1}{\mu \sigma} \frac{2}{r^2} \iff r \ll d \iff \rho \ll 1 \end{split} \tag{17}\] \[
L \approx const.    \qquad
C \approx const.    \qquad
D_S \approx 0
\]


High Frequency: \[\begin{split} & \omega \gg \frac{1}{\mu \sigma} \frac{2}{r^2} \iff r \gg d \iff \rho \gg 1 \end{split} \tag{18}\] \[
L(t) \neq const.    \qquad
C(t) \neq const.   \qquad
\textnormal{for Dipol: } D_S \propto \omega^4 \gg 0
\]


Energy:
Action Quantum, Planck Constant (Energy-Frequency-Slope): 
\[\begin{split} &
h =
\tan(\alpha) =
\frac{E_{kin}}{\nu_i - \nu_0} =
\frac{eV_{0i}}{\nu_i - \nu_0}
\equiv \\&
\frac{E_1 - E_2}{\nu_0} =
\frac{W}{\nu_0} =
\frac{F_{EM} \cdot x}{\nu_0} =
\hbar k \lambda
\end{split} \tag{19}\]
\[\begin{split} &
\hbar =
\frac{E_1 - E_2}{\omega} =
\end{split} \tag{20}\]
\[\begin{split} &
E_{kin}^{max} =
eV_0 =
e \frac{Q}{C} =
h(\nu - \nu_0)
\end{split} \tag{21}\]
\[
V_0 = \textnormal{Opposing Potential ($I=0$)}
\]\[
\alpha = \angle( E_{kin}, \nu ) \qquad
e = \textnormal{electron charge} \qquad
Q = \textnormal{ion charge} \qquad
\]\[
C = \textnormal{Capacitance} \qquad
\nu = \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\sqrt{LC}}
\]\[
\Rightarrow \textnormal{Inductance } L = \frac{4\pi^2}{C} \left( \frac{e}{h}Q + \nu_0 \right)^2
\]


\(\Rightarrow \textnormal{Ionisation Energy of chemical element } E_i = h\nu = h\frac{\lambda}{c} = \hbar\omega = \frac{h}{k\lambda} \omega\)
\(\Rightarrow \textnormal{Engergy released (gained) } E_{gain} = E_{pot} - E_i = mc^2 - h\nu\)
